description_long: This object is a preserved crab specimen with a broad, rounded carapace and two prominent, asymmetrical claws extending forward. The crab's exoskeleton is a pale tan to light brown color, with subtle shading and texture variations across its surface. Its eight walking legs are slender and jointed, radiating outward from the body and ending in pointed tips. The eyes are positioned on short stalks at the front of the carapace, and short antennae are faintly visible. The underside of the crab reveals a segmented abdomen tucked beneath the cephalothorax. The overall appearance is naturalistic, with fine details in the shell texture and limb articulation.
description: A pale tan crab with a rounded shell, two large claws, and eight slender legs, featuring detailed textures and natural anatomical features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the crab's face, both claws raised, and the eyes on short stalks.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the body and the vertical alignment of the claws and legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the segmented underside and the arrangement of the legs and claws from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the crab's profile, with one claw and the curvature of the legs visible.
